behaviors.description: The cron parser rejects invalid expressions (e.g., month 13, day 32, malformed syntax) with a descriptive error message.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is confined to parser validation and error construction at a single module boundary. No real OS/network/subprocess dependency, no external round-trip side effect, and no AI/ML output dependency are stated. Higher levels were considered and rejected: Integration is disqualified because the behavior text does not require parser handoff to another module; System is disqualified because no external contract/side-effect readback is required.
behaviors.test_description: Add focused parser tests that feed invalid cron strings and assert both failure and specific descriptive error text (field + reason).

behaviors.description: The next-run-time calculator returns correct timestamps for standard cron expressions, including edge cases like "last day of month" and "nearest weekday."
behaviors.justification: This is primarily a module-handoff risk: cron expression semantics must be interpreted consistently from parsed schedule representation into timestamp generation. Unit tests per function can miss parser/calculator contract mismatches on edge tokens like last-day and nearest-weekday. System was considered and rejected because behavior text does not require real external API/process or external side-effect readback; Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because no model call is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end in-process tests from cron string -> parse -> next-N timestamp computation, asserting exact expected timestamps for edge expressions across representative start dates.

behaviors.description: The overlap detector identifies when two schedules would fire within the configured minimum gap (e.g., 5 minutes) and reports the conflicting time windows.
behaviors.justification: The likely failure mode is interaction between schedule generation and overlap logic (time-window derivation, gap comparison, reporting boundaries), not a single isolated function. No explicit real OS/network/subprocess requirement or external round-trip visibility is stated, so System is not required. No AI/ML dependency, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Create paired cron inputs and minimum-gap configs, compute firing windows through the normal in-process pipeline, and assert detected conflicts and reported windows match expected boundaries.

behaviors.description: Timezone-aware scheduling produces correct run times across a DST transition — a job scheduled for 2:30 AM handles the spring-forward gap and fall-back ambiguity correctly.
behaviors.justification: DST correctness usually depends on interplay among parser/scheduler/timezone components and date-time library semantics; unit-only tests can miss handoff mistakes around nonexistent/ambiguous local times. System was considered but rejected: behavior text does not explicitly require real external contract checks or externally visible side-effect readback; this can be validated in-process with real timezone rules in the runtime/library. No AI/ML dependency, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Execute in-process tests using a real timezone (e.g., one with DST) and fixed start instants around spring-forward/fall-back, asserting computed run-time behavior for nonexistent and ambiguous 2:30 AM cases.

behaviors.description: When a scheduled job's computed next run time has already passed (e.g., system was down), the engine determines whether to skip or backfill based on the job's configured missed-run policy.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is policy orchestration across components (next-run computation + policy evaluation + scheduling decision), where unit tests can miss state handoff and branch integration errors. System was considered and rejected because the behavior text does not require real subprocess/network/OS contract validation or external side-effect round-trip proof. No AI/ML dependency, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Run in-process scenarios with simulated downtime windows and different missed-run policies, asserting resulting decisions (skip vs backfill) and resulting scheduled run set are correct.
